risk_domain.name: Backup Encryption and Recovery Security
risk_domain.description: Detect weak backup encryption, stale guardians, quantum-vulnerable backup key exchange, lack of coercion resistance, and non-deterministic secrets that cannot be recovered from seed. Protects the recoverability and confidentiality of wallet secrets.

capabilities: full on-chain observation of all encrypted backup data (permanent, public)
offline brute-force against password-encrypted backups (GPU/ASIC acceleration)
future CRQC access for ECDH-encrypted backup key exchange
coercion of user to reveal backup passwords under duress
limitations: no physical access to hardware wallets or secure enclaves
cannot compromise a threshold of independent guardians simultaneously (assumed)

H1_weak_kdf_parameters.name: Weak KDF Parameters
H1_weak_kdf_parameters.description: Use of weak key derivation function parameters that could allow for brute force attacks.

signals.name: weak_kdf_memory
signals.description: KDF parameters configured with insufficient memory cost, indicating potential brute force vulnerability.

signals.name: low_kdf_iterations
signals.description: KDF iterations set to a low number, making it easier for attackers to perform brute force attacks.

recommendations.description: Switch to a stronger key derivation function.
Automatically increase the number of iterations using security software.

H2_stale_guardian_addresses.name: Stale Guardian Addresses
H2_stale_guardian_addresses.description: Lack of recent on-chain activity from guardians indicating potential compromise or inactivity.

signals.name: no_onchain_activity
signals.description: No transactions or interactions with smart contracts from the guardian address.

signals.name: lack_of_recent_staking
signals.description: Guardian has not participated in staking rewards within the last year, indicating possible inactivity or compromise.

signals.name: low_transaction_volume
signals.description: Low transaction volume or frequency from the guardian address over a period of six months.

recommendations.description: Regularly review guardian addresses for on-chain activity.
Set up alerts for lack of recent on-chain activity from guardians.
Periodically update guardian keys to prevent long-term inactivity or compromise.
Encourage guardians to participate more frequently in staking rewards.

H3_quantum_vulnerability_in_backu.name: Quantum Vulnerability in Backup Encryption
H3_quantum_vulnerability_in_backu.description: Backup uses encryption methods vulnerable to quantum attacks due to reliance on elliptic curve cryptography.

signals.name: ecdsa_signature_length
signals.description: Length of ECDSA signatures used in backup encryption is less than 128 bytes.

signals.name: elliptic_curve_used
signals.description: Elliptic curve algorithm (e.g., P-256, Curve25519) is known to be vulnerable to quantum attacks.

signals.name: kdf_parameters_weak
signals.description: Key derivation function (KDF) parameters are below recommended minimums, e.g., iterations <1000 or memory <64MB.

recommendations.description: Migrate to quantum-resistant encryption algorithms such as NewHope or NTRU.
Increase KDF iterations and memory parameters to recommended minimums (e.g., >1000 iterations, >64MB).
Deploy PQC algorithms in your encryption process.
Ensure the elliptic curve used is not vulnerable to quantum attacks and update if necessary.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Test Quantum Vulnerability in Backup Encryption

H4_no_coercion_resistance.name: No Coercion Resistance
H4_no_coercion_resistance.description: Single key backup or deterministic password-to-seed mapping, making it easier for attackers to derive the correct seed under duress.

signals.name: single_backup_key
signals.description: Backup ciphertext decrypts to the correct plaintext using exactly one valid key.

signals.name: deterministic_password_seed
signals.description: The same password always produces the same seed, without honey encryption protections.

signals.name: decryption_errors_on_wrong_passwords
signals.description: Incorrect passwords result in decryption errors rather than plausible fake seeds.

recommendations.description: Enable honey encryption to prevent attackers from distinguishing correct seeds.
Store backup ciphertexts encrypted with multiple keys to deter coercion.
Use different password-to-seed mappings for each user session to avoid deterministic patterns.
Deploy honeypot keys that produce incorrect seeds but still decrypt to gibberish.

H5_non_deterministic_secrets_not_.name: Non-Deterministic Secrets Not Backed Up
H5_non_deterministic_secrets_not_.description: Failure to back up non-deterministic secrets generated during transactions, leading to loss of state consistency.

signals.name: non_deterministic_secret_generation
signals.description: Detection of transactions interacting with protocols that are known to generate non-deterministic secrets.

signals.name: missing_backup_transaction
signals.description: Identification of non-deterministic secret generation without an on-chain backup transaction being submitted.

signals.name: recovery_method_discrepancy
signals.description: Detection of wallets claiming seed-only recovery while using protocols that generate non-deterministic secrets.

recommendations.description: Manually back up non-deterministic secrets generated in each transaction.
Automatically send backup transactions to a smart contract for secret storage.
Regularly audit wallet recovery methods against current protocol usage.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Test Non-Deterministic Secrets Not Backed Up

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring backup security posture across all recovery dimensions

methodology.step_1: Inventory all backup and recovery configurations (password, guardians, hardware keys, on-chain backups)
methodology.step_2: For each backup, assess: encryption strength, KDF parameters, PQ safety, deniability, guardian liveness
methodology.step_3: For non-deterministic protocol interactions, verify backup existence
methodology.step_4: Score overall backup security as composite of brute-force resistance, recoverability, and coercion resistance

combined_benchmark.target: All backups: PQ-safe encryption, multi-factor, liveness-checked guardians, deterministic or explicitly backed up secrets

adversary_levels.name: opportunistic
adversary_levels.description: Brute-force password-only backups with commodity hardware

adversary_levels.name: targeted
adversary_levels.description: Target specific user: brute-force + social-engineer guardians + coercion

adversary_levels.name: quantum_capable
adversary_levels.description: All attack vectors including CRQC against ECDH-encrypted backups

templates.summary: Backup security scan: {n_txs} configurations analyzed. {n_risky} at risk ({pct_risky}%). Guardian liveness: {guardian_liveness}%. Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.summary_clean: All {n_txs} backup configurations pass security checks. Guardians active, encryption PQ-safe.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This improves backup security by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
templates.guardian_alert: Guardian {guardian_address} has been inactive for {inactive_days} days. Recovery threshold at risk: {active_guardians}/{total_guardians} active vs {threshold} required.
templates.backup_warning: WARNING: This backup uses {encryption_scheme} which is {vulnerability}. Recommended: {recommendation}.
